summary.intent: Build and iterate on a browser-in-browser feature for the Moltis agent platform, enabling users to view, interact with, and manage browser sessions directly from the settings UI, while agents share the same browser profiles for cookie persistence.
summary.outcome: Delivered a fully functional browser viewer with live CDP screencast, mouse/keyboard/scroll interaction, URL autocomplete, session history with action logs (SQLite), per-agent browser profiles with cookie sharing via tab reuse, and comprehensive bug fixes for session stability. 50+ commits merged into PR #531.

repo: BrowserTool and RealBrowserService had separate BrowserManager instances — sessions created by the tool were invisible to the UI service; fixed by sharing an Arc<OnceCell<BrowserManager>>
CDP Page.startScreencast relay exits when tx.send() finds 0 receivers (drops-on-no-receiver semantics); must use send() that ignores errors instead of returning Err when there are no subscribers
WebSocket event subscription filter (broadcast.rs:100-102) silently drops events not in the client's subscription list — browser.screencast.frame was missing from websocket.js subscription list
serde #[serde(default)] only handles missing fields, not explicit null; LLMs like MiniMax send null for all optional fields including u64 types, requiring a null-stripping step before deserialization
SingletonLock in Chrome profiles is a symlink to the container hostname; exists() follows symlinks and returns false for dangling symlinks, so cleanup requires symlink_metadata() instead
CDP mouseWheel requires both deltaX and deltaY always present — omitting either field when zero causes 'deltaX and deltaY are expected for mouseWheel event' error
Screencast frame metadata contains scroll_offset_x/scroll_offset_y and offset_top; offset_top must be added to y-coordinates for accurate click positioning
Chrome profile locking means only one Chrome process per profile; multiple sessions with the same profile_id should share a single browser instance (tabs) rather than launching new containers
Action hook on BrowserManager must be registered inside the OnceCell init closure, not at startup — the manager is lazily initialized and the hook would be lost if set before first use
Container browser sessions hit ERR_NETWORK_CHANGED on first request after startup; retry with the same parameters resolves it
biome 0.3.3 (npx biome) and biome 2.4.6 (system biome) produce different formatting — local-validate.sh uses the system binary, so always format with the system binary

code.finding: Original relay task exited when tx.send() returned Err (no receivers), causing 'waiting for first frame' forever — CDP sends first frame before UI subscribes
null-stripping via obj.retain(|_, v| !v.is_null()) is placed after session_id and sandbox injection in execute()
action_name from Display format is 'mouse_input(x=129.7, y=231.5)' not 'mouse_input' — starts_with() required for matching, not exact equality
Browser field changed to Arc<Browser> to enable tab sharing across BrowserInstance entries with the same profile_id
default_sandbox flag injected into all UI browser requests via RealBrowserService::request(); configured from config.tools.browser.sandbox

canvasRefCallback (ref callback pattern) must be used instead of useEffect([]) for attaching canvas event listeners — the canvas is conditionally rendered, so useEffect with empty deps fires before canvas exists
EventFrameNavigated provides URL change events; frame.parent_id is Option but frame itself is not Option in the CDP bindings

workflow: YubiKey GPG signing blocks background git commits; interactive foreground commits work but require user presence
biome --write only applies fixes when run directly; CI runs biome --check without --write, so fixes must be committed separately
local-validate.sh 531 is the correct way to validate a PR before pushing; catches biome, fmt, i18n, and install-name issues
Axum 0.8 changed route parameter syntax from :param to {param}
Cargo workspace deny(unused-qualifications) and deny(clippy::expect_used) apply in test code too — use unwrap_or_else patterns in tests
Running cargo clippy on all features fails on CUDA crates on macOS without nvcc; run clippy only on affected crates to avoid noise
CDP screencast coordinate mapping: use img.naturalWidth/naturalHeight for scale factors, not hardcoded values or metadata device dimensions

friction: YubiKey GPG signing required multiple retries throughout the session — background tasks timed out waiting for tap, requiring repeated foreground retry attempts
biome version mismatch (npx biome 0.3.3 vs system biome 2.4.6) caused repeated CI failures after local --write appeared to fix issues
Session crash bugs had to be diagnosed iteratively through user-reported logs across many iterations before root causes were isolated
The 'waiting for first frame' bug required 3 separate fixes (relay exit, WebSocket subscription, navigate URL normalization) before it was fully resolved
Click accuracy required multiple iterations: offsetX/offsetY fix, then aspect ratio fix, then offset_top metadata correction
GatewayServices initialization order required significant refactoring — browser service created before db_pool, requiring lazy hook registration via OnceCell
axum route syntax change (:param → {param}) caused a runtime panic only discovered after deploy, not caught at compile time
Chrome profile SingletonLock as a dangling symlink was not caught by the cleanup code for multiple sessions, causing ~45-second startup timeouts
open_items: WebRTC upgrade (neko/GStreamer) for lower latency (<100ms) and better bandwidth efficiency vs CDP JPEG screencast — tracked as future PR
Container memory/disk usage display in Settings > Containers page — deferred as separate feature
E2e Playwright tests for full UI interaction flow (session create/switch/close lifecycle, cookie persistence, scroll/click accuracy) — 13 Rust unit tests added but Playwright suite incomplete
Multiple Chrome profiles in one container for memory efficiency — determined not feasible with Chrome's profile locking model
Per-session cookie isolation when needed (agents that should NOT share UI cookies) — currently all agents use 'default' profile
Cookie persistence test for concurrent sessions — logged in session cookies not always visible to new tab if Chrome hasn't flushed to disk
Scrollbar thumb size and position accuracy depends on evaluate() for scrollHeight — only queried once per navigation, may be stale on dynamic pages
Browser session history action log UI is minimal — no replay, no screenshots per action, no timeline visualization
Container health monitoring / memory pressure detection to automatically switch to host mode — config option exists but no automation
